perturbation.original_text: This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and other persons , if expressly permitted by staff of FNHA and those discussions are conducted on the terms and c onditions that FNHA may direct.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and other persons.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the condition "if expressly permitted by staff of FNHA and those discussions are conducted on the terms and conditions that FNHA may direct.", the clause now suggests that discussions with other persons are unrestricted. This contradicts the earlier part of the section that says the company will not disclose, discuss, publish or disseminate Confidential Information to a third person, as it now allows for these discussions to occur without condition. This perturbation makes communicating with the media, in relation to the confidential information or any information with respect to the RFP, valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non -Disclosure. Company will not make copies of , disclose, discuss, publish or dissemi nate Confidential Information any third person or entity. Company will not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than responding to the RFP. Company will take reasonable precautions to secure and prevent any unauthorized use, disclosure, publication or disseminatio n of Confidential Information. Company may not at any time directly or indirectly communicate with the media in relation to the Confidential Information or any information with respect to the RFP without first obtaining the written permission of FNHA. Comp any acknowledges and agrees that nothing contained in this Agreement will be construed as granting it any rights, by license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information. Company agrees to return Confidential Information to FNHA. This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and FNHA staff or with any other persons identified b y FNHA as having undertaken this Agreement. This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and other persons.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes a key condition regarding discussions of Confidential Information with other persons. The original text allowed these discussions 'if expressly permitted by staff of FNHA,' whereas the changed text removes this condition, suggesting that discussions with other persons are now unrestricted. This creates a direct conflict with section 2, which states that the company will not disclose, discuss, publish, or disseminate Confidential Information to any third person. The removal of the condition allows for potential uncontrolled communication of confidential information which directly breaks the clause of Non-Disclosure agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Equitable Relief. Company acknowledges that all of the Confidential Information is owned solely by FNHA and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information would cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, Company agrees that FNHA will have the right to obtain an immediate injunction enjoining any breach of this Agreement, as well as the rig ht to pursue any and all other rights and remedies available at law o r in equity for such a breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Equitable Relief. Company acknowledges that all of the Confidential Information is owned solely by FNHA and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information would cause irreparable harm and significant injury. Accordingly, Company agrees that FNHA will have the right to pursue any and all other rights and remedies available at law o r in equity for such a breach.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain", the clause now removes the implicit difficulty of quantifying the damage. This creates an in-text contradiction, because it implies that damage is easy to ascertain. This can create an in-text contradiction because the first remedy that the document states FNHA has a right to, is to obtain an immediate injunction. Having the degree of difficulty to ascertain, removed, makes it more difficult to obtain an immediate junction. This perturbation makes the first right/remedy of an immediate injunction, not possible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Equitable Relief. Company acknowledges that all of the Confidential Information is owned solely by FNHA and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information would cause irreparable harm and significant injury. Accordingly, Company agrees that FNHA will have the right to obtain an immediate injunction enjoining any breach of this Agreement, as well as the rig ht to pursue any and all other rights and remedies available at law o r in equity for such a breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the degree of harm may be difficult to ascertain, which supports the need for an immediate injunction. By removing this phrase, the changed text implies that the degree of harm is easily ascertainable, weakening the justification for an immediate injunction, thus contradicting the continued assertion of the right to an immediate injunction in both the changed text and the contradicted text. The explanation provides that this 'makes the first right/remedy of an immediate injunction, not possible'. This is a strong in-text contradiction because the initial justification for the injunction is undermined by the change.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Confidential Information and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements concerning such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements concerning such Confidential Information.", this clause no longer explicitly invalidates prior agreements. This is in direct contradiction with the intent to have this agreement as the single source of truth and any other prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements can be considered valid and in effect.  The modification impacts the entire premise of the document, which is that this document is the final say. The contradiction exists in section 1. Definition. That is a Key Term for the whole agreement to function.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Definition. "Confidential Information" means any information identified as confidential by the FNHA that is part of the FNHA’s request for proposal number 2019RFP-02, Architectural & Design Team Consultants for Metro Vancouver Office Project (“RFP”) whether in oral, written electronic or any other form or medium whatsoever, including questions answered or information disclosed by FNHA related to confidential aspects of the RFP or discussions held related to the RFP.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the clause that supersedes prior agreements related to Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' in section 1, which refers to information within the context of the RFP. Without the superseding clause, prior agreements could potentially conflict with the current agreement's definition and scope of Confidential Information, creating a direct contradiction in the understanding and enforcement of what constitutes confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance w ith the laws of the Province of British Columbia , without regard to or application of choice -of-law rules or principles and the parties submit to the jurisdiction of BC courts.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance w ith the laws of the Province of British Columbia.
perturbation.explanation: By removing ", without regard to or application of choice -of-law rules or principles and the parties submit to the jurisdiction of BC courts.", the clause now fails to specify the exclusive jurisdiction of BC courts. This opens up the possibility of conflicting jurisdictions being applicable if other clauses or external laws suggest otherwise. The contradiction is with Section 4's equitable relief. The remedy in this clause is that it gives the power to FNHA to obtain an immediate junction enjoining any breach of this agreement. With this new clause modification, this now makes it uncertain as to what court could issue that injunction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Equitable Relief. Company acknowledges that all of the Confidential Information is owned solely by FNHA and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information would cause irreparable harm and significant injury. Accordingly, Company agrees that FNHA will have the right to pursue any and all other rights and remedies available at law o r in equity for such a breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that disputes would be resolved in BC courts, establishing a clear jurisdiction. The changed text removes this specification. Section 4 grants FNHA the right to pursue equitable relief, including injunctions. Without the specification of BC courts, it becomes unclear which court has jurisdiction to issue such an injunction, creating a contradiction and uncertainty where the original clause provided clarity. As such, this adheres to the omissions definition, where language is removed and this creates contradiction with the other section's constraints.
